title: Netavark doesn't yet support --isolation=oci with buildah v1.44.0
body:
### Issue Description

Trying to use `--isolation=oci` with buildah version 1.44 results in:

```
Error: building at STEP "RUN echo hi": setup network: netavark: failed to load network options: IO error: invalid type: sequence, expected a map at line 1 column 101
```

According to Claude-assisted investigation, this is a result of https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/689 not yet being supported in netavark. The support is in main (https://github.com/containers/netavark/commit/49ee09b249077b3f89d8f9d483b2c340e6f6cd54), but not in any tag.

Not sure if this repo is the right place for this issue, please redirect me if needed.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Compile buildah at v1.44.0 (`make bin/buildah`)
2. Create minimal `~/.config/containers/storage.conf` if needed to account for the config changes:
    ```bash
    echo -e '[storage]\ndriver = "overlay"' >~/.config/containers/storage.conf
    ```
3.
    ```bash
    $ bin/buildah build --isolation=oci -f - <<EOF            
    FROM fedora-minimal:42
    RUN echo hi
    EOF

    STEP 1/2: FROM fedora-minimal:42
    STEP 2/2: RUN echo hi
    error running container: did not get container start message from parent: EOF
    Error: building at STEP "RUN echo hi": setup network: netavark: failed to load network options: IO error: invalid type: sequence, expected a map at line 1 column 101
    ```

### Describe the results you received

Error from netavark

### Describe the results you expected

Working build with OCI isolation

### buildah version output

```yaml
Version:         1.44.0
Go Version:      go1.25.9 X:nodwarf5
Image Spec:      1.1.1
Runtime Spec:    1.3.0
image Version:   5.40.0
Git Commit:      30a4189415e5d0a3a0c5971307a6432d8ed1a097
Built:           Wed Jun  3 14:40:30 2026
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

### buildah info output

```yaml

        },
        "MemFree": 5308596224,

        "SwapFree": 8589930496,
        "SwapTotal": 8589930496,
        "arch": "amd64",

        "hostname": "acmiel-thinkpadx1carbongen12.tpbc.csb",
        "kernel": "7.0.9-102.fc43.x86_64",
        "os": "linux",
        "rootless": true,
        "uptime": "5h 35m 25.1s (Approximately 0.21 days)",

    },
    "store": {

        "GraphImageStore": "",

        "GraphStatus": {

```

### Provide your storage.conf

```toml
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: I expect there's a new netavark release that will go with this one, as the upcoming podman 6 builds use the same container-libs release.

There isn't an upstream release _currently_, but I expect there will be by the time everything is packaged as RPMs. This problem likely comes from us packaging the upstream buildah release in a container rather than waiting for RPMs.

It would be nice if changes like this happened in the opposite order, where netavark would support the new syntax first and buildah would switch to it a few releases down the line. But if RPMs are the intended consumption mechanism and we're doing something unsupported, then I'm OK with facing breakages like this every once in a while
Folks are also running into #6880 while releases are not quite in sync.

> It would be nice if changes like this happened in the opposite order, where netavark would support the new syntax first and buildah would switch to it a few releases down the line. But if RPMs are the intended consumption mechanism and we're doing something unsupported, then I'm OK with facing breakages like this every once in a while

I mean I do not disagree, frankly the nv release should have been out weeks ago but I was caught up in totally critical CI work that had to happen first.

Generally speaking the current containers-common, buildah,podman,rpm and netavark rpms all have to be released in one update in sync as they all depend on each other basically.

I guess the release notes should make this more clear.

Anyhow I Assume I should be able to work on finishing up the nv/av release work this/next week so we should have something out by next week I hope.
